fact: March 2 is exactly 61 days into the year, which means there are exactly 304 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, March 2 falls smack in the middle of when maple trees start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes maple syrup!
March 2 typically marks the peak of 'kitten season' when outdoor cats start having babies because spring is coming in northern climates!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around March 2 in most places, so you're literally gaining daylight faster than you can blink!
March 2 sits right in the sweet spot where people born on this day are Pisces - the fish sign that's supposedly the most creative and dreamy of all zodiac signs.
Earthworms often start wiggling up to the surface around March 2 in warmer places because the soil is finally thawing out from winter!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, March 2 is when the leaves start turning brilliant orange and red as autumn kicks in!
March 2 falls exactly 18 days before the spring equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal lengths all over Earth!
Robins in North America typically start building their nests around March 2, using mud as glue to stick twigs together - nature's construction workers!
The average temperature on March 2 usually jumps up by about 10 degrees compared to February 2 in most cold places - winter is officially losing the battle!
March 2 marks when hibernating bears often start stirring in their dens in northern regions, getting ready to wake up hungry and grumpy after months of sleeping!
Dandelions - those yellow 'weeds' that drive adults crazy - start poking their heads up around March 2 in warmer climates, ready to take over every lawn!
